task_title: MVP Decomposition for Taiwan Stock Quant Research and Backtesting System

tasks.title: Bootstrap architecture and core interface scaffolding
tasks.description: Create MVP-first repository/module skeleton and typed interfaces for all major layers: data acquisition, cache/storage, canonical schema, strategy, signal pipeline, selection pipeline, backtest core, batch runner, reporting, and tests. Focus on contracts, config stubs, and dependency boundaries; no runtime-heavy implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Project structure contains modules for each required layer with placeholder files/classes
Core abstract interfaces/protocols are defined and importable without circular dependencies
Basic configuration and dependency wiring stubs exist, and tests can discover modules

tasks.title: Universe abstraction for Taiwan stocks
tasks.description: Define a stock-universe domain model and provider interface supporting full TW universe, symbol identity, metadata fields, and effective-date snapshots.
acceptance_criteria: Universe model includes symbol, exchange/market, listing status, and update timestamp
Universe provider interface supports full snapshot retrieval and point-in-time access
At least one mock/in-memory universe provider is available for tests

tasks.title: Data provider interface contracts
tasks.description: Define pluggable market/chip/flow data provider interfaces with unified method signatures, error model, pagination, and rate-limit metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Provider interfaces cover OHLCV and chip/flow/market-structure data categories
Common request/response contracts include symbol, date range, and granularity
Interface-level tests validate provider contract behavior via fakes

tasks.title: Raw fetch module skeleton
tasks.description: Implement a non-production raw fetch orchestration module that calls provider interfaces, handles retries/backoff policy contracts, and emits raw payload artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Raw fetch orchestrator accepts provider implementation via dependency injection
Retry/backoff and error classification hooks are present and testable
Raw payload persistence contract is invoked with metadata for traceability

tasks.title: Local cache layer
tasks.description: Build local cache abstraction and initial file/db-backed implementation for raw and normalized datasets with keying, versioning, and read/write APIs.
acceptance_criteria: Cache API supports put/get/exists/list for symbol-date partitions
Cache entries include schema version, source, and created/updated timestamps
Unit tests verify deterministic cache keying and retrieval behavior

tasks.title: Incremental update workflow
tasks.description: Add incremental update planner/executor that computes missing date windows per symbol and updates cache without full refetch.
acceptance_criteria: Incremental planner computes deltas from last successful watermark per dataset
Executor updates only missing partitions and preserves idempotency
Tests cover first-load, no-op update, partial-gap fill, and retry-safe rerun

tasks.title: Canonical normalization format
tasks.description: Define canonical schema for market and chip/flow records, implement normalization mappers from raw provider payloads, and enforce schema validation.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical models specify required fields, dtypes, timezone/date semantics, and null policy
Normalizer transforms provider-specific payloads into canonical records
Validation catches malformed records with explicit error messages

tasks.title: Strategy base interface
tasks.description: Create strategy base contracts for parameter schema, required data dependencies, signal generation entrypoints, and metadata (name/version/tags).
acceptance_criteria: Strategy interface defines standardized input context and output signal schema
Parameter validation contract exists for runtime-safe strategy initialization
A minimal example strategy compiles and runs against synthetic data

tasks.title: Technical strategy support module
tasks.description: Implement technical-indicator strategy support primitives and at least one reference technical strategy adapter using the strategy base interface.
acceptance_criteria: Technical feature computation utilities are modular and reusable
At least one technical strategy (e.g., MA crossover) emits canonical signals
Tests validate deterministic signals on fixture data

tasks.title: Chip/flow strategy support module
tasks.description: Implement chip/flow/market-structure strategy support primitives and at least one reference strategy adapter using canonical chip/flow fields.
acceptance_criteria: Chip/flow feature utilities are separated from technical feature utilities
At least one chip/flow strategy emits canonical signals with parameterization
Tests verify expected signal outcomes on controlled fixtures

tasks.title: Signal pipeline orchestration
tasks.description: Build signal pipeline to run selected strategies over prepared datasets, manage execution context, and output timestamped per-symbol signals.
acceptance_criteria: Pipeline supports running one or multiple strategies with clear execution order
Signal output format is canonical and strategy-identifiable
Pipeline tests cover empty inputs, mixed strategy sets, and error isolation

tasks.title: Stock selection pipeline
tasks.description: Implement selection layer that filters/ranks symbols from signal outputs and universe constraints to produce tradable candidate lists.
acceptance_criteria: Selection API supports filter rules, ranking rules, and top-N selection
Selection respects universe membership and data availability constraints
Tests validate ranking and tie-break behavior

tasks.title: Backtest core engine
tasks.description: Implement backtest core loop for portfolio state transitions: order simulation, position accounting, cash/equity updates, and event timeline.
acceptance_criteria: Engine processes entry signals into simulated orders and positions
Portfolio accounting tracks cash, holdings, PnL, and equity curve consistently
Unit tests validate state transitions on deterministic scenarios

tasks.title: Exit and risk rule constraints
tasks.description: Add optional exit/risk constraints: stop-loss, take-profit, max holding period, and rule-composition handling.
acceptance_criteria: Exit rules are configurable and composable per strategy/backtest run
Engine enforces stop-loss/take-profit/holding-period consistently
Tests cover conflicting triggers and priority resolution

tasks.title: Batch backtest runner
tasks.description: Create batch execution module to run many symbols/strategies/time windows with reproducible run IDs, checkpointing hooks, and summary aggregation.
acceptance_criteria: Batch runner accepts parameter grid and symbol universe subsets
Each run emits metadata, status, and deterministic artifact paths
Failure isolation allows partial completion with error reports

tasks.title: Reporting outputs and artifacts
tasks.description: Implement reporting layer for run summary, trades, equity curve, drawdown, and strategy-level metrics in machine-readable and human-readable outputs.
acceptance_criteria: Reporter outputs at least JSON/CSV artifacts for core metrics and trades
Summary includes key KPIs (return, drawdown, win rate, turnover)
Report generation is decoupled from backtest engine internals

tasks.title: System tests and validation harness
tasks.description: Add layered test suite and validation checks spanning interfaces, normalization, pipelines, backtest logic, and batch/reporting integration.
acceptance_criteria: Test suite includes unit, contract, and integration coverage across all layers
Validation fixtures cover Taiwan-universe-specific edge cases and missing data
CI-ready test command passes with deterministic fixtures

instruction: Next step: SEND_TO_PLANNER for T01. Planner should produce an implementation-ready spec for foundational architecture and interface scaffolding only, with concrete module/file map, contracts, and acceptance-test plan while avoiding runtime-heavy data fetching/backtesting work.
summary: Work is decomposed into an MVP-safe sequence from low-risk foundational scaffolding through data pipeline, strategy pipeline, backtest core, batch execution, reporting, and validation, covering all required capability areas.
next_action: Route T01 to Planner for detailed spec and execution plan.
